title: Residualized tire-degradation state features
summary: A new 2026 F1 paper models tyre wear as a latent state with pit stops as resets, which matches this task closely.
rationale: A new 2026 F1 paper models tyre wear as a latent state with pit stops as resets, which matches this task closely. Your current features use raw degradation ratios, but not whether a driver is degrading faster than expected for that race/compound/stint regime.
implementation_hint: Estimate target-free baseline pace/degradation curves by Year-Race-Compound-Stint or Year-Race-Compound-TyreLife bins using only training folds. Add residual features such as LapTime_Delta minus race-compound median at same tyre-life, cumulative degradation minus expected curve, and short-term acceleration of that residual. Treat pit stops as regime resets.
expected_effect: Should separate genuinely 'falling off the cliff' tyres from normal old-tyre behavior, which is exactly where next-lap pit probability spikes.
risk: Fold-safe residualization is mandatory; if baselines are fit on full data, leakage will inflate CV.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
